On Fri, Jan 24, 2025 at 2:29 PM Stephanie McCauley <
stephanie@ismilestudios.com
> wrote:
Hi Julie, sorry for the delay in responding, we just moved our studio into a new building yesterday and life is upside down right now ha ha.
Middle of February is great for us, you can pretty much choose the date and time that works for you.  I would anticipate needing an hour of shooting time, with 30 minutes set up prior and 30 minutes breakdown after (so please reserve your room for 2 hours) but we will only need to give students a 1 hour window of time to be photographed.
We can do the photos according to the specifications for $15 per student (this way, instead of a flat fee for all of them, you are not stuck paying for kids that don’t show up).  We will deliver the digital files within 24 hours of the shoot and guarantee acceptance by any government agency needing them.  We will also keep an archival copy on our side, should you ever need them again and are unable to find them.
So, if this is all ok, we can compare calendars!  We won’t bill you until after we know how many subjects we had, and at that time I will make sure you have the W9 paperwork you need!
Thank you so much!
Steph
On Jan 23, 2025, at 8:39 AM, Harris, Julie <
harrisj@union.edu
> wrote:
Good morning!  Thanks for the quick reply.
We only need digital formats so that students can upload them with applications.
Our plan is to offer this service to students one time only - sort of a "one and done" and if they miss out, they have to get this done on their own.  We can reserve a room for however long you anticipate needing and schedule students according to your recommendation for time per student.  I will take care of sign ups.  It would be most convenient if we planned this for the middle of February - maybe the week of the 10th which gives us time to notify students and get sign-ups.
Thank you for your time.  May I ask, are you already a vendor with Union?  If not, I'll need to get a W-9 so that we can get you set up.
Look forward to hearing from you,
Julie
On Wed, Jan 22, 2025 at 7:40 PM Stephanie McCauley <
stephanie@ismilestudios.com
> wrote:
Hi Julie!
We would be so happy to help you with this project.
I read over the specifications for the images and can absolutely make the headshots fit this criteria.  The only thing I couldn’t find was whether they need them printed (if so, what size and how many prints) or if they need them in digital form?
Would it be possible to photograph all these students at the same time?   I am sure we can take care of all of them within 30 minutes or so.    This would make it much more affordable for you.   Also, we would have to decide ahead of time how to handle any students who don’t show up - will they just miss it or be required to do a make up photo, and if so, where?
Once I have this information I can come up with a quote and we can pick a date!
Thanks so much!

Hi Stephanie,
It's nice to meet you over email.  I received your name from Julie Brannon at the Career Center.  
I work with international students.  In March, our senior class members will start applying for what is called Optional Practical Training to stay in the states and work in the US.  Part of the application requires an updated headshot.  I've attached the guidelines that need to be followed for this.
Might you have time in your schedule to help us schedule these photos for students?  We expect maybe 20-25 to participate and we'd like the photos to be complete by March 1.
Thank you for your consideration.  Look forward to working with you.

Subject:
 Re: Headshots

Hi Tom!
Thank you so much for the opportunity to quote this job!   
We would need about 2 hours to complete all the headshots and group photo for approximately 25 people.  If helpful I can create an online signup form with 5 minute intervals so people can come in a little more organized manner and not stand around waiting.   We can arrange for the group photo either at the beginning or the end of our time there.
I would recommend bringing studio strobe lighting and taking the headshots on a neutral background.  This way, I can provide you consistent well-lit images of everyone regardless of the ambient lighting in the office or the changes that occur over a two hour period with daylight.   Further, I can provide you with both the neutral background as photographed AND remove that background and put an environmental scene behind them so you have that as an option, too.
Our price is $800 and includes the following: 
2 hours on site (plus additional time to set up and break down, 30 minutes on each end)
Optional online sign up if desired 
At least five images of each staff member 
All images organized by staff, artfully retouched, in full resolution with a commercial copyright release (each staff member will have their own online gallery and you will receive links to all of them plus another one with all the files in one place)
All files provided in original form (neutral background), plus transparent (no) background, plus environmental background if desired
Here is a quick example of our work, I wouldn’t necessarily use a green backdrop as our neutral unless it matched your color palette but so you can see an example:
Smile Lodge Doctor Headshots June 2024 - iSmile
ismile.smugmug.com
Standing by if you have any questions or you are ready to schedule a date. 
Thank you SO MUCH! 
Sincerely, 
Steph

historical_picture_day_info.summary: Joanne Casey Bridal Shower - (Lauren R. & Steph)
historical_picture_day_info.picture_day_info:
Hi Lauren,

It is so nice to meet you. Stephanie, thank you so much for the introduction. I so appreciate you both and Lauren thank you so much for being there on March 22. This bride-to-be is actually my future daughter-in-law, Sarah. Stephanie was gracious enough to be there for my daughter Lauren’s bridal shower back in 2023. The pictures were amazing, and I am so happy you will be there for my son’s future wife. The shower starts at 1 pm, so Lauren if you would like to get there at 12:30 pm that would be great, and leaving at 3:30 sounds good – I completely trust your judgement so when you feel as though you have enough, please feel free to take off. Also, we will plan on you having brunch with us so if you have any dietary restrictions, please let me know and I will handle it.

My dear friend, Stephanie is so kind to do this for us again. I value our friendship more than you know (and therefore she is stuck with me no matter what and I will never forget about her - 😊). Lauren, my cell phone is 518-376-7417. Please let me know if you need anything.

Thank you, thank you, thank you! You guys are the best!

Joanne
Hi Steph,

I am so excited to have you there on Saturday – you have no idea! Lauren is so excited as well. Arriving at 12 pm is perfect and the whole plan sounds amazing. Please come hungry as we are serving brunch and the food is supposed to be excellent. We will be at the Edison Club - 891 Riverview Road, Rexford, NY 12148.  I believe you have my cell phone but just in case – it is 518-376-7417.

Thank you so much!

Joanne
Hi Stephanie,

As always, it was great to see you today. I love working with you!  I wanted to follow-up to see about you coming to my daughter’s bridal shower. Lauren’s shower is being held on Saturday, April 15 from 12:30 pm – 4:30 pm at the Edison Club in Clifton Park.  I would love to have you there for either all or part of it. Let me know what you are thinking in terms of cost and we can go from there.

Also, send me the link for the Google Review – any time I can sing your praises, I am ready. Thanks so much.
Joanne

Joanne Casey

Hi Brittany!
We got your email regarding doing a family photo fundraiser - we would LOVE to set something up for you this spring!!!!
As you may know from the other Ballston Spa PTAs - we charge each family a $25 sitting fee that is 100% donated to your PTA.  That includes the session and one digital file of the family.   People can optionally buy more photos or digitals but are not obligated to in any way.
Sometimes it is good to pair this with a holiday so people have a “reason” to do family pics - Valentine’s Day is great.  Easter is also an option although we get pretty busy in the weeks leading up to that so scheduling might be a little tougher then.
We do all the work to organize appointments (we do it online) - all you have to do is promote it.
What questions can I answer, or what dates should I check for you?

Hi Sharon!
That is so exciting!  Congratulations on your growing family <3 
We have the date available for the baby shower and would love to provide the photo booth.   Our booth does not print - is that ok?  Instead it sends images via text or email right away.    We can either set it up facing a wall, or (my preference) facing out into the shower to have the party vibe in the background.
We can also create a custom border that includes a caption/date etc and we can incorporate the colors you are using if you want.
The booth is $150 per hour with a 2 hour minimum, so $450 for 11 - 2.
Alternatively, we can send a photographer to do professional photos of your shower - that would include posed and candid pictures with the mom to be with all her guests.  That is also $150 per hour and you would receive all the digital files.  If you choose that route, we recommend doing the first two hours of the event, it gets very repetitive after that - so your fee would be $300.
Basically, you’ll want to decide if you want to have a fun photo booth to play with unattended and guests can do silly pictures - or if you would want professional photos instead.
Standing by!!!!

Steph

Potential Event Description: 
A panel discussion featuring three to four local and nationally known female entrepreneurs/leaders. The panelists should come from a variety of career fields (science, business, arts) and have diverse backgrounds and perspectives. The panel will touch upon challenges faced as a woman during your first career after college and strategies for success. It will also discuss various paths to leadership as a woman. The event will be student-focused and will include a variety of interactive activities to connect students with the panelists. 
Following the event, students will have the opportunity to mingle with panelists and have their resumes reviewed, grab some professional attire from the Free-Use Closet, and have some free headshots taken.

historical_picture_day_info.summary: Emerging Leaders Conference AM only STEPH
historical_picture_day_info.picture_day_info:
As the Chair of the Emerging Leaders Committee and a proud Member of the Board of Directors, I am excited to share some updates on our recent activities and invite you to join us for our upcoming events. Our inaugural Emerging Leaders Summit in October 2023 was a tremendous success, featuring phenomenal and influential speakers providing valuable professional development to over 50 attendees. This success has inspired us to host more impactful programs.
I am thrilled to announce our 
second annual Emerging Leader Conference, which will be held on Thursday, October 17, 2024, from 8 to 11:45 am at The Century House in Latham
. This event promises to be an enriching experience for leaders at all stages. To ensure you don't miss out, be sure to join our Emerging Leaders Committee email list to be the first to know when registration opens.
My journey with the Colonie Chamber — from Marketing Communications Specialist right out of college to serving on the Board of Directors and leading the Emerging Leaders Committee — has been deeply rewarding. I have seen firsthand how leadership development is vital for our community's growth, and I am grateful for the Chamber's support in uniting individuals behind this mission. Your participation is invaluable, and I look forward to seeing you at our events.
Have a warm and wonderful summer, 
Brittany

1/15/25 2:53 PM (16 minutes ago)
to me
Good Afternoon Carrie,
Thank you for following back up with me! 
We appreciate iSmile Studios every year at the RPI Spring Career Fair! 
This year, the career fair will be held on Wednesday, February 12 from 11 AM to 3 PM.
We can have a table and chairs available! 
As far as location, we hold the career fair in the Armory on RPI's campus.
The Physical address is 1616 15th St, Troy, NY 12180
The Armory will open to employers at 9:00 am for setup. Please do not arrive earlier than 9:00 am.
I am sharing the information about parking below from our Employer Relations Team!
Please let me know if you have any questions for the day.
Parking will be in B Lot on campus, across Peoples Ave from the Houston Field House.
Shuttles will be running beginning at 9:00 am between the covered bus shelter at B Lot and the Armory to bring you to and from the Career Fair.
If you need to drop off large display items or materials at the Armory before parking in B Lot, you may drive to the Rear Armory Parking Lot and drop these off any time between 9:00-11:00 am. Volunteers will be available to help unload cars and bring materials to your company's tables while you park. (NOTE: Following GPS directions to the Armory / Alumni Sports & Recreation Center will lead you to the front entrance, where you will NOT be able to unload.
Please navigate to the Rear Armory Parking Lot for drop-offs only, and then to the B Lot for parking. There will also be clear signage on campus to all locations!) Please note you will need to enter this lot via Griswold Rd, NOT via 15th Street - only the Griswold Rd parking gate will be open for visitors.
Thank you and have a great day!
Sincerely, 
Rawdon Marroquin (He/Him/El)

Assistant Director, Career Development 
Liaison for the School of Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences
Coordinator of DEIB Initiatives

7:00 AM (2 hours ago)
to Holli, Holli, me, Molly, Marianna
Hi Holli,
I hope you are doing well! I'm reaching out today regarding Mother Teresa's upcoming spring picture day on Tuesday, April 29th. Molly and I will be your photographers, with our colleague Marianna assisting.
From our notes, I see we will be inside with spring backdrops for individual student photos. We will arrive at 9 and begin photographing students at 9:30. Since we will be indoors, I wanted to check if you have chapel in the morning since I am assuming that is the room we will be setting up in. If so, do we need to adjust our planned arrival and start times? Also, if there is a schedule you could share with us, that would be great! If we are able to have a table or desk available in the room for us to use, that would be helpful as well.
We look forward to seeing everyone next week! Have a great day and please let me know if you have any questions.
Best,
Beth
--
Beth Picciano
Photographer
www.ismilestudios.com

Hi Matt,
Thank you! We are so excited and GRATEFUL that you are able to partner with us for this event. This is our annual big and small winter ball - big = adult and small = school age (Pre K - 5th) child. It is our hope this year to offer every child and their adult free admission; we’ll charge a small admission fee for any extra adult(s). 
The date is January 25th at the Ravena Coeymans Selkirk (RCS) High School on Route 9W in the main gym from 6pm - 8pm. 
Let me know if you need an approximate headcount. I can look at the guest list from last year or we can wait until rsvp’s roll in closer to the event. 
Regards,
Kathy Blenis

historical_picture_day_info.picture_day_info:
Hi Steph, 

Wow, three day parent orientation?! That seems like overkill to me, haha!

I’m comfortable with that price point and greatly appreciate the discount. My hope is this is just the beginnings of an all-star collaboration between us!

To confirm, we’re thinking of that Thursday, Sept 5? (Just confirming because there are orientations before that.) I would LOVE to capture happy smiling faces getting off the school bus. The timeline terrifies me, but let me run it past the directors to see what they think. I agree that it could potentially be a good day to do it, before people are 
really 
in the swing of things. Let me reach out and get back to you ASAP.

I will definitely provide a very specific, detailed shot list – to the minute, lol I’m a liiiittle crazy in that regard. 😊  I’ll draft something for you and then lets work together to finalize based on how much time you think each setup will need.

I’m very open to trying to find time for portraits on portrait day, if you are. I was thinking with those that we’d stage them (students and teachers) in a more natural classroom environment (hallways, chalk/whiteboard behind them, etc.).

Just confirm with me that you were thinking 9/5 and I’ll talk to the directors ASAP.

Thank you!!
Kayla

From: 
Stephanie McCauley <
stephanie@ismilestudios.com
>
Date: 
Thursday, August 15, 2024 at 8:47 AM
To: 
Kayla Germain <
GermainK@albanyacademies.org
>
Subject: 
Re: Shoot
Hi Kayla!

Thank you so much for your patience as I got my son settled into college. If you can believe it, there was a three day parent orientation! So different from when I went to school ha ha.

I can’t wait to collaborate with you on this exciting project. My regular hourly rate is $350, but I would gladly discount that to $2000 for the full day for you.  This price is all inclusive, including all fully edited high resolution digital files with a commercial copyright release delivered within 48 hours of the shoot.

The trick may be finding a full day to make this happen, because I am already fully committed through December. We could try to move some stuff around if you had a specific target date in mind. I was also wondering if maybe the first day of school might work? Sometimes, things look the very best on that day and we would be less disruptive to teachers doing actual academic work  Just a thought and I can definitely clear that day for you. 

In my experience, if you have a list of very specific shots that you want, it’s usually better to stage those with cooperative students rather than try to get them organically. 

Regarding formal portraits, perhaps we can handle this on regular school picture day which is I believe September 11, I will have to double check on that but that’s close. There would be no extra charge for any of that, we have all of our lighting set up, and can do either before school, after school, or during a break.  I will be at school the entire week of August 28 photographing seniors, so we could squeeze stuff in there too if you want. 

I will also provide your office with professional portraits of all staff and students for you to use any way you need to. 

I know I just threw a lot at you, but hopefully this can get us thinking and moving in the right direction to get everything set up.

Standing by!!!  ❤️

Steph

The 2024-2025 Special Event Coordinators

Mon, Sep 15, 2:53 PM (18 hours ago)
to CJ, me
Hi CJ!
I had a second to draft a schedule for the photos.  
To be truthful this is a VERY aggressive timeline/schedule for one person, which is all I have available for this date.  (I do have someone doing the team photos after I am done with the individuals but only me taking all the individuals.)   This will result in two things:
1.  I will be limited to just a couple poses per kid, which should be ok because I can turn a full body into a close up and change to black and white etc so there will still be variety, but 
bigger …
2.  
Every single solitary team needs to come FIVE MINUTES EARLY, lined up ALPHABETICALLY, with a ball (unless I can grab one from you to use all day) and have a coach WITH ME who is able to “feed” me the kids one at a time and then corral them after for their team pic immediately after their individuals.   This is the ONLY way this will work using just one individual photographer.  It has to be locked in with every team all day.
Do you think everyone is capable of this?  Because, if they aren't, I WILL run behind and then everyone will be mad and then games will be delayed and if someone is late at the start it will domino all day long.  Thinking about that scenario, it would be better to not even do photos than to cause that level of disruption.
Thoughts?
Not trying to be Negative Nancy, just know from vast experience what this can devolve into and acknowledging that this is a LOT of kids for one person.   If EVERYONE is five minutes early with their players lined up alphabetically, it can be done.

Hi Carrie, 

I hope you are doing well.  I am reaching out to see if iSmiles Studios would be available to provide professional headshot services at our upcoming Capital Region Careers Networking Night on Wednesday, June 25, 2025, from 6:00PM – 8:00PM, at the Hefner Alumni House on the RPI campus.  

This event is an opportunity for RPI students to connect with local Capital Region employers who are promoting internships and co-op opportunities.  I believe adding a professional headshot service would be a valuable opportunity, giving students an important resource as they build their professional image.

Please let me know if you are available and if you have any questions regarding logistics, space, or requirements you may have.  I would love to collaborate with you on making this a great opportunity for our students. I am looking forward to hearing from you. 

Dean Burke

9 hours at Turning Stone  $800 including set up.   Steph working on this with Paula Rohl via Honeybook.  
Yep, that works for us! You’ll be set up in the ‘Expo’ which will be in the Turning Stone Event Center.

Notes (unsure if you are familiar with TS):
Parking at Turning Stone is free – both the garage & valet – 3
rd
 floor of the garage is the same level as our event. If you can’t park on 3 – then you can take the elevators to the 3
rd
 floor. We will have signage directing folks to the event/Atrium area
The Expo will be in the Event Center – where they hold concerts, etc.
There is free coffee/soda in the casino – soda fountain & coffee bar
There is also a Starbucks ($) at the bakery – which is by the food court
We also will have a “Hospitality” booth – with food donated from our members – please help yourself! All kinds of flavored milks, yogurts, chips, snacks, jerky, cheese curds, etc. and open all day
Lunch is a buffet – please help yourself to that as well.

I’ll try & remind you of this as we get closer but if not, here it is!
Hi! 

Here are my initial thoughts - certainly subject to your opinion and changes are fine!

Headshots usually start off busy and wind down as it gets later in the day, mostly because people get tired and don’t want to be photographed when they don’t feel their best.

Maybe we could do 9:00 am start (I would be setting up at 8:30) as the keynote is finishing, and wrap up around 2:30 (after the 2:00 break)?

Happy to modify if you think we need different timing!    (I can do about 30 people per hour, I don’t know how many attendees you will have … usually about 20-25 percent do headshots unless it’s mandatory lol)

Sincerely,
Paula

On Feb 10, 2026, at 6:45 AM, Kelli Rushia <
Kelli.Rushia@gmail.com
> wrote:
I’m Stephanie from iSmile Studios and I am so excited to receive your request!!!
We would absolutely LOVE to work with your league, and I have tentatively reserved your date (April 25) so it’s safe while we work out all the particulars.  No obligation or pressure.
I am not sure how familiar you are with our program … so I will give you some initial information but please let me know if you have any specific questions!
We offer a seamless, well run picture day, photographing all your individual players and teams.  We take several poses of each player so parents have choices, and we love to do a couple different team photos too (time permitting).  There is no pre-ordering sight unseen - instead, we create a gallery for each player with a unique access code and parents receive a link via email for their daughter.
You guys can pick from natural light images (we love shooting in dug-outs and that works even in the rain) or we can do something indoors with studio lighting if you prefer that.    We can work together to come up with a schedule based on how many teams you have and how many teams of photographers we will bring, so no team has to wait around too long on Opening Day.  
We can help you with team/sponsorship banners too, either in the design of them or the printing of them (when you work with us we print those banners at cost and it will save you TONS).
If it isn’t too late, we would also love to sponsor a team!
I am happy to hop on a google meet to walk you through our process and answer any questions or strategize the perfect solutions for your league!    I have a hectic day tomorrow and Wednesday but Thursday and Friday are more open if you have time.
Thank you so much for this opportunity!
Sincerely,
Steph

Hi Stephanie,
I hope you are doing well! We would like to set up a photo session for our graduating international students to take pictures for their work authorization applications. We have about 30-35 students who need pictures. I'm including Heidi Hodges, who is our new international coordinator, on this message so she can work with you on getting everything set up.
The photo specs are the same as last year:
The photos must be 2 x 2 inches
Direct-front-facing view of the face
Head height should measure 1 to 1 3/8 inches from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin
Head Position: 50% to 69% of the total image height
Eye height should measure between 1 1/8 to 1 3/8 inches from the top of the eyes to the bottom of the photo
Color-correct, no filters, no re-touching
Digital files should be in JPG format and file names should not include any punctuation or symbols
Digital files should be 600x600 to 1200x1200 pixels, ideally between 54 KB and 10 MB 
Background: Plain white or off-white with no shadows or patterns
Restrictions: No glasses, no hats/head coverings (except for religious), no headphones
Our office will cover the cost for all of the photos, so please let us know an approximate cost for the photo session. We can confirm specific numbers for you soon!
Thanks,
Robin

calendar_import_candidates.picture_day_info:
Hi!!!  ❤️
This is all 100% perfect, it’s a GO!
Matt has great energy and everyone will love having him take their pictures.  He usually gets somewhere between 5-8 poses for people - kind of depends on if they are male or female and if they have hair or accessories that can be changed to add variety.
Rest assured ALL photos will be free for ALL attendees, delivered in full resolution with gentle artful retouching added.  We will give you a master list of everyone at the end, too.
And - we want to work with you all the time - so the BOGO is a small way for us to thank you for this and all future opportunities!!!!!
Matt will reach out to you a few days prior to confirm everything and share his cell number with you in case you need him day of.
For background options - we have a pretty blue, or a nice corporate gray, or black, or white, or a forest green.  I would say blue (keeping with the ACC color palette) or gray since it’s generic and useable anywhere.   No rush on deciding that.
Thank you sooooo much and I am so sorry to miss it - next time for sure!!!
:)
Steph
On May 30, 2025, at 11:28 AM, Shannon Licygiewicz

Hello Stephanie and Hi Matt!!! 

Thanks so much for getting back to me with this info!! Let’s dive in a little bit!
I am SO bummed to hear you are not going to be able to join us on June 12
th
 – but am looking forward to meeting Matt! Please know this will be a group of fun and chatty hospitality folks so we will be high energy and just (hopefully) bubbly all day! 
Love that you stalked the show!!! Let me clarify what is going to happen – the tradeshow portion of the event runs from 2pm-4pm that’s when everyone will be on the floor…I am actually going to set you guys up in the ACC’s tradeshow booth space. So, attendees and even other exhibitors can come and get their headshots. Will be a fun way to interact with attendees and offer an epic take away!
While I am so very appreciative of the buy one gets one hour free – there is absolutely NO WAY I am only paying you only $350.00 for this!!! Please let me compensate you some more for the ability to have the attendees get their photos for free!!!! I do not want anyone needing to pay for the downloads so please let me know if there are any additional charges I can help with. 
Matt not sure if you have a sweet tooth but we will also be offering a candy bar in our booth!! 

Look forward to finalizing things and again THANK YOU for agreeing to do this – I think it is going to be FUN!

Hi Shannon!

Wanted to touch base with a few more details on this awesome opportunity for us!

The first thing is - I am of course booked at 3:00 (right in the middle of your event) but I met with my amazing colleague Matt this morning and he is available to do this for you - which is way better than having me there so it all worked out ha ha!   (Matt and I work very closely together and he has a very similar style, you will love him.  He’s copied on this.)

Alternatively, if you want to change the time we will be there with you, I am personally available anytime before 1:00 that day.   (I stalked the event and see that it runs 8-5, I think your choice of 2-4 pm is a good one where the max amount of people will be milling around, but happy to adjust if needed.)

From our experience, the best way to do this is to give each person a unique code for their photos, take a variety of poses, and then upload them and give them the code which allows unlimited free downloads of their pictures.  It also captures their name and email so you’ll have a list of everyone that stopped at your booth and did photos.

Our normal price is $350/hour but I would love to give you a buy one get one free hour, so for the two hours there we would only charge $350.  We would arrive about 45 minutes prior to our start time to get set up.  

Hopefully that anticipated most of your questions and it fits into the budget.  

Standing by to strategize!  Thank you so much!

Steph

Hello Kristina,
Thank you for reaching out about our photobooth for your 2025 prom. We have revised our prom packages and I think you'll be pleasantly surprised. We changed things up significantly to include ALL photos to the kids, so no one has to pay for anything once you book us - all photos are included. The easiest way to do this is to just divide the cost by the number of tickets and work it into the price - should end up being less than $10 a ticket and that’s way less than having to buy photo packages. You'll see that the photo booth can be added to our packages, and can be personalized with the prom date, school name, etc.
iSmile Prom Pricing 2024.jpeg
We do have June 7, 2025 available and because we love your school community, I can hold the date until a decision is made.

Have the BEST day,

Mon, Sep 15, 2:53 PM (18 hours ago)
to CJ, me
Hi CJ!
I had a second to draft a schedule for the photos.  
To be truthful this is a VERY aggressive timeline/schedule for one person, which is all I have available for this date.  (I do have someone doing the team photos after I am done with the individuals but only me taking all the individuals.)   This will result in two things:
1.  I will be limited to just a couple poses per kid, which should be ok because I can turn a full body into a close up and change to black and white etc so there will still be variety, but 
bigger …
2.  
Every single solitary team needs to come FIVE MINUTES EARLY, lined up ALPHABETICALLY, with a ball (unless I can grab one from you to use all day) and have a coach WITH ME who is able to “feed” me the kids one at a time and then corral them after for their team pic immediately after their individuals.   This is the ONLY way this will work using just one individual photographer.  It has to be locked in with every team all day.
Do you think everyone is capable of this?  Because, if they aren't, I WILL run behind and then everyone will be mad and then games will be delayed and if someone is late at the start it will domino all day long.  Thinking about that scenario, it would be better to not even do photos than to cause that level of disruption.
Thoughts?
Not trying to be Negative Nancy, just know from vast experience what this can devolve into and acknowledging that this is a LOT of kids for one person.   If EVERYONE is five minutes early with their players lined up alphabetically, it can be done.

I’m Stephanie from iSmile Studios and I am so excited to receive your request!!!
We would absolutely LOVE to work with your league, and I have tentatively reserved your date (April 25) so it’s safe while we work out all the particulars.  No obligation or pressure.
I am not sure how familiar you are with our program … so I will give you some initial information but please let me know if you have any specific questions!
We offer a seamless, well run picture day, photographing all your individual players and teams.  We take several poses of each player so parents have choices, and we love to do a couple different team photos too (time permitting).  There is no pre-ordering sight unseen - instead, we create a gallery for each player with a unique access code and parents receive a link via email for their daughter.
You guys can pick from natural light images (we love shooting in dug-outs and that works even in the rain) or we can do something indoors with studio lighting if you prefer that.    We can work together to come up with a schedule based on how many teams you have and how many teams of photographers we will bring, so no team has to wait around too long on Opening Day.  
We can help you with team/sponsorship banners too, either in the design of them or the printing of them (when you work with us we print those banners at cost and it will save you TONS).
If it isn’t too late, we would also love to sponsor a team!
I am happy to hop on a google meet to walk you through our process and answer any questions or strategize the perfect solutions for your league!    I have a hectic day tomorrow and Wednesday but Thursday and Friday are more open if you have time.
Thank you so much for this opportunity!
Sincerely,
Steph

Hi Stephanie,
I hope you are doing well! We would like to set up a photo session for our graduating international students to take pictures for their work authorization applications. We have about 30-35 students who need pictures. I'm including Heidi Hodges, who is our new international coordinator, on this message so she can work with you on getting everything set up.
The photo specs are the same as last year:
The photos must be 2 x 2 inches
Direct-front-facing view of the face
Head height should measure 1 to 1 3/8 inches from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin
Head Position: 50% to 69% of the total image height
Eye height should measure between 1 1/8 to 1 3/8 inches from the top of the eyes to the bottom of the photo
Color-correct, no filters, no re-touching
Digital files should be in JPG format and file names should not include any punctuation or symbols
Digital files should be 600x600 to 1200x1200 pixels, ideally between 54 KB and 10 MB 
Background: Plain white or off-white with no shadows or patterns
Restrictions: No glasses, no hats/head coverings (except for religious), no headphones
Our office will cover the cost for all of the photos, so please let us know an approximate cost for the photo session. We can confirm specific numbers for you soon!
Thanks,
Robin

We can give you a half day (4 hours) at $500 or a full day (8 hours) at $800.   We can add drone at no additional cost, too, but it does take time away from still shots since there’s a little set up involved as well as flying time.  But - an option.  Might be fun to have some video or high altitude stills of kids crossing, if they are still doing that?
I put this on my calendar and blocked off the whole day, you can decide even last minute what it all will look like.   Thank you so much for the opportunity!
:)
Steph

Hi!!!  ❤️
This is all 100% perfect, it’s a GO!
Matt has great energy and everyone will love having him take their pictures.  He usually gets somewhere between 5-8 poses for people - kind of depends on if they are male or female and if they have hair or accessories that can be changed to add variety.
Rest assured ALL photos will be free for ALL attendees, delivered in full resolution with gentle artful retouching added.  We will give you a master list of everyone at the end, too.

Hi Shannon!

Wanted to touch base with a few more details on this awesome opportunity for us!

The first thing is - I am of course booked at 3:00 (right in the middle of your event) but I met with my amazing colleague Matt this morning and he is available to do this for you - which is way better than having me there so it all worked out ha ha!   (Matt and I work very closely together and he has a very similar style, you will love him.  He’s copied on this.)

Alternatively, if you want to change the time we will be there with you, I am personally available anytime before 1:00 that day.   (I stalked the event and see that it runs 8-5, I think your choice of 2-4 pm is a good one where the max amount of people will be milling around, but happy to adjust if needed.)

From our experience, the best way to do this is to give each person a unique code for their photos, take a variety of poses, and then upload them and give them the code which allows unlimited free downloads of their pictures.  It also captures their name and email so you’ll have a list of everyone that stopped at your booth and did photos.

Our normal price is $350/hour but I would love to give you a buy one get one free hour, so for the two hours there we would only charge $350.  We would arrive about 45 minutes prior to our start time to get set up.  

Hopefully that anticipated most of your questions and it fits into the budget.  

Standing by to strategize!  Thank you so much!

Steph
